title: T0-04 — Make LiveView submit non-blocking
description: Primary files: lib/muse_web/live/home_live.ex, lib/muse/session_server.ex, lib/muse/session_router.ex, lib/muse.ex. Add non-blocking submit API such as submit_async/start_submit returning {:ok, turn_id} or {:error, :turn_in_progress}; keep sync API with finite timeout and single-flight guard; HomeLive submit must use async API and rely on PubSub events for progress/completion; UI assigns should reflect submitting/active turn immediately and clear on terminal events.
acceptance_criteria: Browser submit returns immediately after startup; LiveView can process PubSub, cancel, approval, and diagnostics during long turns; UI shows durable running state; turn_completed/turn_failed/turn_cancelled clears running state; turn_in_progress produces clear warning; LiveView tests cover immediacy and state transitions.
close_reason: T0-04 implemented: LiveView submit is non-blocking. Browser submit returns immediately, submitting?/active_turn_id assigns track turn state, terminal events clear running state, turn_in_progress shows warning toast, sync submit API preserved for CLI/TUI. 24 new tests all pass.

title: T0-02 — Add browser LiveView access control or loopback-only enforcement
description: Primary files: lib/muse_web/router.ex, lib/muse_web/endpoint.ex, config/*.exs. Ensure the browser LiveView coding control surface is not reachable remotely by accident. Enforce loopback-only local/dev binding or explicit browser authentication; production must require secret/session auth unless explicitly trusted-loopback; unsafe combinations such as 0.0.0.0 plus no auth must fail closed with clear config errors.
acceptance_criteria: Default local dev remains convenient on loopback; remote browser access is not accidentally available; production requires explicit auth or trusted loopback-only binding; misconfiguration produces clear error; router/plug and runtime config tests cover reject/allow paths.
close_reason: Implemented and tested. Browser LiveView access control enforced via loopback-only plug. Runtime config validation rejects unsafe combos. 57 new tests, all 4652 tests pass. Pushed to main.

title: T0-03 — Prevent concurrent submissions from corrupting active turn state
description: Primary files: lib/muse/session_server.ex and callers of Muse.submit/3 or SessionRouter submit APIs. Implement single-flight reject policy while a turn is active: finite submit timeout, turn_active?/1 guard, shared start_turn function, consistent clear_turn_state, structured logs for stale results/DOWN messages, and internal submit_rejected event. Return {:error, :turn_in_progress} quickly for concurrent submit.
acceptance_criteria: Second submit during active turn does not mutate active_turn_id or original caller; first caller receives correct reply; second caller gets {:error, :turn_in_progress} quickly; stale task results are logged and ignored without active-state mutation; submit timeout returns {:error, :submit_timeout}; lifecycle tests cover success/failure/cancellation/stale/concurrent cases.
close_reason: Closed

title: T0-05 — Convert provider streaming to true live event emission
description: Primary files: lib/muse/conductor.ex, lib/muse/conductor/tool_loop.ex, lib/muse/session_server.ex, runner startup path. Replace process-dictionary/buffered replay streaming with live emit_event_fn callback. Emit Muse.LLM.Event deltas immediately as provider callbacks arrive, append/publish only for current active turn, prevent duplicate replay at completion, and safely ignore or mark stale late cancellation events.
acceptance_criteria: First assistant delta reaches PubSub before provider completion; LiveView can render streaming text while provider call is active; completion does not duplicate deltas; stale turn_id event specs are ignored; tool-loop provider calls use same live streaming path; fake provider tests prove before-completion PubSub delivery.
notes: elixir-code-critic review: applied small hardening fix for T0-05 live streaming. SessionServer now ignores malformed live event specs safely and suppresses further live deltas after cancellation_requested; added regression tests in conductor_streaming_test. Focused tests/format/compile pass.

description: Primary files: lib/muse_web/channels/user_socket.ex, lib/muse_web/channels/session_channel.ex, lib/muse_web/external_socket_config.ex, config/config.exs, config/runtime.exs. title: Provider: handle wafer textual tool-call stall
description: When wafer.ai / GLM-5.1 receives OpenAI tools/tool_choice params, it doesn't return structured tool_calls but emits textual tool-call markers (like thinking/reasoning about tools). Muse then treats the content as final assistant text and stalls. Fix: (1) Add MUSE_TOOLS env / supports_tools capability flag enforcement in ModelPreparer so tools are omitted when provider doesn't support them, (2) Update launcher to set MUSE_TOOLS=false for wafer, (3) Add defense-in-depth sanitizer in Conductor to detect raw tool-call markers in content when no structured tool_calls exist.
close_reason: Already fixed by commit 0c4c6ee — duplicate/companion of muse-5c3, same commit.

title: Release readiness audit for Muse Runtime v0
description: Final release-readiness pass for Muse Runtime v0. This audit verifies documentation accuracy, CLI/TUI/web smoke tests, build verification, quality gates, and security review before v0 release.
acceptance_criteria:
- README quickstart and provider docs match current behavior
- CLI smoke works on fake provider
- TUI/help/command surfaces are discoverable or documented
- LiveView/web route smoke is build/test-covered or manually smoke-tested if feasible
- Escript build works and CLI executable responds to non-interactive help/version/status where available
- Release packaging can be assembled or documented if not appropriate for current env
- Full gates pass (mix format, compile warnings-as-errors, mix test)
- No secrets or unsafe remote-execution defaults are exposed
notes: Blocking epics muse-1ki.4 and muse-1ki have been closed. This is the final pre-release verification.
close_reason:
Release readiness audit complete. All acceptance criteria verified:

## Files Inspected
- README.md (quickstart, provider config, CLI commands, safety model documented)
- docs/security.md (comprehensive security checklist, all items checked)
- docs/provider-roadmap.md (provider config contract documented and matches README)
- mix.exs (version 0.1.0, escript and release config correct)
- config/config.exs, config/runtime.exs (external WS disabled by default, secret key base required in prod)
- lib/muse/cli/main.ex, lib/muse/cli/release_command.ex (entrypoints working)
- lib/muse/cli/tui.ex (TUI documented with help popup)
- lib/muse_web/router.ex (LiveView route at /)
- lib/muse/execution/policy.ex, lib/muse/execution/local_runner.ex (remote execution denied, safe argv-vector execution)

## Gate Results
- `mix format --check-formatted`: PASSED
- `mix compile --warnings-as-errors`: PASSED
- `mix test`: PASSED (3605 tests, 0 failures)

## Build Verification
- `mix escript.build`: SUCCESS - generated `muse` escript
- `./muse --help`: SUCCESS - prints usage and exits 0
- `./muse --no-web` with `/help`, `/quit` piped: SUCCESS - CLI responds correctly
- `mix phx.routes`: SUCCESS - LiveView route at /, external WebSocket at /socket (disabled by default)
- `MIX_ENV=prod mix release`: SUCCESS - release assembled at _build/prod/rel/muse/
- Release `bin/muse_cli --help`: SUCCESS with MUSE_SECRET_KEY_BASE set

## Security Verification
- Remote execution denied by policy (Muse.Execution.Policy)
- Local runner uses argv-vector Port.open, no shell interpolation
- Output capped and redacted, no secrets leak
- External WebSocket disabled by default, read-only when enabled
- Production requires MUSE_SECRET_KEY_BASE (>= 64 bytes)
- .gitignore excludes _build/, muse escript, erl_crash.dump

## Limitations
- No browser-based LiveView smoke (not feasible in non-interactive audit)
- TUI mode requires ExRatatui NIF; escript mode correctly rejects --tui with clear error
- Test suite has deprecation warnings about single-quoted strings (test-only, not runtime)

## Verdict
Muse Runtime v0 is RELEASE READY. All gates pass, security model is enforced, CLI/escript/release builds work, documentation matches behavior.

title: Harden streaming collector for async provider callbacks
description: Why: T0-05 live streaming still collects final provider event specs with process-dictionary state in Conductor/ToolLoop. In-tree fake/SSE paths call emit callbacks synchronously today, but the Provider/sse_post_fn/ws_stream_fn contracts do not explicitly require same-process callback execution. If a future transport invokes the emit callback from another process or after provider return, collected specs/delta indexes can be lost or crash, causing missing diagnostics or inconsistent dedup. What: replace or wrap collector state with a process-safe mechanism (e.g. parent-owned collector process/message aggregation) or formalize/enforce the same-process callback contract with tests.
acceptance_criteria: Async callback invocation from a spawned process cannot crash Conductor/ToolLoop; final event_specs and live-emitted delta dedup remain correct; same-process provider behavior remains unchanged; tests cover Conductor and ToolLoop/final provider paths.
notes: Post-review elixir-code-critic-3f89ac: added late-callback hardening so StreamCollector.record/2 and mark_live_emitted/1 treat calls after collect/1 as safe no-ops instead of uncaught GenServer.call exits; added regression test. Focused streaming tests and compile pass.
close_reason: Replaced process-dictionary streaming collectors with Agent-based Muse.Conductor.StreamCollector. All 3 call sites (Conductor.stream_provider, ToolLoop.run_iteration, ToolLoop.final_provider_call) now use StreamCollector, which is safe across process boundaries. 14 unit tests + 4 integration tests for async provider callbacks. 189 Conductor/ToolLoop/streaming tests pass with 0 failures.

title: T1-18 — Optimize tool loop CPU, concurrency, and token/API usage
description: Primary files: lib/muse/conductor/tool_loop.ex, lib/muse/conductor.ex, lib/muse/prompt/project_rules.ex, lib/muse/prompt/assembler.ex, and large-output tools. Replace repeated accumulator ++ with reverse accumulation; classify tools as read-only/idempotent vs write/apply/approval/stateful; run independent read-only tools with bounded async_stream_nolink while preserving order; keep write tools serial; centralize summarize_for_model caps for bytes/keys/list/depth/string; cache prompt/project rules by path/mtime/size/config with invalidation.
acceptance_criteria: Accumulators avoid growing-list ++; read-only tools complete near max latency under bounded concurrency; write/apply/approval tools remain serial; model-facing tool output centrally bounded with explicit truncation metadata; prompt cache reuses unchanged content and invalidates on change; tests cover order, concurrency, serial writes, summarization, truncation marker, and cache invalidation.

Replace File.read/String.split JSONL loading with line streaming; preserve invalid-line/skipped diagnostics; add SessionStore.find_patch/3 targeted lookup; write imports/exports incrementally via File.open/IO.write; avoid duplicate startup I/O where practical.
acceptance_criteria: JSONL load memory is line-bounded; invalid lines are skipped/reported as before; find_patch locates patch without loading unrelated patches; import/export handle large sessions without giant intermediate strings; tests cover many lines, invalid skipped count, find_patch, and one-line-per-entry writer.

Store per-session events in bounded queue/equivalent; track event_count explicitly; build per-turn lists in reverse and reverse once; replace state.events ++ session_events with centralized bounded append; convert to chronological lists only at public/read API boundaries.
acceptance_criteria: Appending events is O(new events), not O(total history); session event retention bounded; status event count O(1); consumers still receive chronological events; tests cover cap, order, event_count after drops, and long append sequence bounds.

Replace recursive eager traversal and repeated concatenation with lazy stream/resource walking, lazy file-pattern filtering, early stop at max_results, remaining-capacity per-file search, reverse accumulation, safe inaccessible-directory handling, and preserved symlink/path safety.
acceptance_criteria: repo_search stops after max_results; does not materialize full tree before first results; inaccessible dirs do not crash; large repo memory use is bounded; tests verify early stop, no full materialization, safe inaccessible dirs, and no repeated result concatenation hotspot.

Limit stdout bytes, fail on oversized output, trim and validate token shape/length, apply timeout, and use secret-safe environment without logging token output.
acceptance_criteria: Huge bearer command stdout cannot exhaust memory; oversized output fails safely; timeout returns explicit error; secret output is not logged; valid small token output succeeds; tests cover huge stdout, timeout, and valid token.
notes: Post-review fixes by elixir-code-critic-9b64f2: preserved resolver auth_runner arity-2 contract; added safe invalid-runner/non-binary/invalid-UTF8 handling; removed command text from exec lookup errors; passed bearer timeout/max stdout aliases through resolver; focused auth tests pass.
close_reason: T1-09 Bound bearer command stdout completed. System.cmd/3 replaced with port-based bounded execution. max_stdout_bytes (default 4096) prevents memory exhaustion. Env.port_env sanitizes child environment. ProcessGroup.terminate_group kills full process tree on timeout/oversized output. Token shape validation rejects non-printable output. 47 tests pass (20 new). Secret output never logged.

close_reason: T1-14 completed. All per-session events, command history, streaming buffers, toasts, and diagnostics are now bounded by configurable caps via Muse.Bounds. Streaming buffers clear on turn_completed, turn_failed, and turn_cancelled. 32 new tests cover cap enforcement, terminal event cleanup, UTF-8 buffer trimming, and app-env overrides. title: Muse Runtime v0.2.0
description:
## Objective
Post-v0 hardening, polish, and foundation after the safe v0.1.0 release. v0.2.0 focuses on making Muse production-ready in terms of release/distribution QA, UX resilience, provider robustness, persistent session foundations, and observability.

## Context
v0.1.0 delivered the complete Muse Runtime v0 vision: read-only Planning Muse, approval gates, patch proposal/checkpoint/rollback, Coding/Testing/Reviewing Muses, real providers (OpenAI SSE/WebSocket, Anthropic, Ollama, OpenRouter), CLI/TUI/LiveView integration, session persistence, memory/restoration Muses, secure remote-execution-denied runner, and comprehensive docs.

v0.2.0 is the post-v0 hardening release. No new architectural milestones — only polish, resilience, multi-workspace foundations, and release-readiness.

## Scope
- Release/distribution CI polish (artifacts, checksums, install docs, CI automation)
- LiveView/TUI/CLI UX hardening and browser QA
- Provider/model routing UX and resilience (health, error UX, retries, external tests opt-in only)
- Persistent memory/session and multi-workspace foundations
- Remote execution approval design spike (design only unless separately approved)
- Observability, docs refresh, and release readiness

## Non-Goals
- No new Muse roles or specialist profiles
- No remote execution implementation (design spike only)
- No cloud sync or SaaS infrastructure
- No major UI redesign
- No breaking changes to the public API (Muse.submit/2)
- No MCP server/ecosystem integration
- No evaluation harness or cost tracking (backlog items)
- No autonomous shell loops or browser automation

## Safety Invariants (MUST be preserved)
- Fake provider remains the default test provider
- Remote execution remains denied by default
- Secrets never appear in prompts, logs, events, or provider debug output
- All approval gates remain enforced
- Workspace/file safety is never weakened
acceptance_criteria:

- [ ] External provider tests exist but are opt-in (not default)
- [ ] Fake provider remains the default test provider
- [ ] Durable local session/memory store works across restarts
- [ ] Session export/import works securely
- [ ] Multi-workspace profiles/switching work without leaking state
- [ ] Remote execution design document exists; remote execution remains denied by default
- [ ] Telemetry/event export works without leaking secrets
- [ ] Troubleshooting and debugging docs exist
- [ ] v0.2.0 release is tagged, release notes published
- [ ] All safety invariants: fake-provider-first, remote-denied-by-default, secrets-never-leaked, approvals-enforced, workspace-safety

title: Fix PR24 LocalRunner non-zero exit handling before relying on git apply
description:
Final verification of PR24 (`muse-1ki.4.5`) found a blocking correctness/security issue in the local execution runner integration.

Evidence:
- `lib/muse/execution/local_runner.ex` returns `Result.ok(..., exit_status: status)` for non-zero process exits (currently around lines 217-223 and 257-263), so `result.status` remains `:ok` even when the command failed.
- `lib/muse/tools/patch_apply.ex` `run_git_command/2` treats any `%ExecutionResult{status: :ok}` as `{:ok, ""}` without checking `exit_status` (currently around lines 425-430).
- Therefore `git apply --check` and `git apply` failures can be interpreted as success. `PatchApply.apply_via_git/3` may then mark a patch `:applied` and checkpoint `:active` even though git rejected the patch.
- The existing PR24 test `test/muse/execution/local_runner_test.exs` asserts this non-zero-as-ok behavior, and PR18 patch apply tests do not cover a `git apply --check` failure path after approval/checkpoint creation.

Why it matters:
- Patch apply's safety contract depends on `git apply --check` failing closed before writes and on actual `git apply` failures being reported. Misclassifying non-zero exits undermines that checkpoint/approval boundary and can create false audit records.

Suggested fix:
- Either make `LocalRunner` return a `Result.error/3` (or equivalent non-`:ok` status) for non-zero exit statuses while preserving captured output, or update every caller to gate on `exit_status == 0` explicitly.
- Update `PatchApply.run_git_command/2`, `GitStatus`, and `GitDiffReadonly` to fail on non-zero exits.
- Add regression tests for LocalRunner non-zero semantics and for an approved patch whose `git apply --check` fails, asserting the patch is not marked applied and the checkpoint is not active.
acceptance_criteria: LocalRunner represents non-zero exit statuses as failed results or all callers consistently check exit_status; PatchApply treats git apply --check/apply non-zero exits as errors and does not transition patches/checkpoints to applied/active; GitStatus/GitDiffReadonly return errors for non-zero git exits; regression tests cover invalid git apply and non-zero LocalRunner exit semantics.
close_reason: Fixed: LocalRunner non-zero exits now produce Result.error (status: :error) instead of Result.ok. PatchApply.run_git_command/2 fails closed on non-zero exit. TestRunner preserves test-failure-as-verification-failure semantics. GitStatus/GitDiffReadonly already handled correctly via status: :ok pattern match. All 3605 tests pass. Regression test added for git apply --check failure not marking patch as applied.

title: Security: render canonical memory safely when artifact list fields are malformed
description: PR21 muse-avz made Memory.render/1 recursively redact values inside canonical list fields, but the renderer still assumes each field is Enumerable. A persisted canonical memory artifact whose expected list field is replaced by a tuple/scalar can crash Memory.render/1 before it renders a safe summary. Confirmed with MIX_ENV=test mix run --no-start -e using project_facts: {:password, "field-sentinel"}: Memory.render/1 raises Protocol.UndefinedError. More importantly, Exception.message(e) includes the raw tuple under a "Got value" section (e.g. {:password, "field-sentinel-3"}). CommandDispatcher.format_memory/1 rescues render failures and embeds Exception.message(e) in the /memory output, so malformed stored memory can leak raw sensitive values in the display error. Prompt.Assembler also treats canonical memory as Muse.Memory.render/1 without a rescue, so provider-bound prompt assembly can crash on malformed stored memory instead of producing a redacted/withheld summary.
acceptance_criteria: Memory.render/1 does not raise for canonical memory artifacts when any expected list field is nil, a scalar, map, tuple, keyword list, charlist, or deeply nested malformed term. Values under sensitive atom/string keys remain redacted and rendered output contains no raw sentinel secrets. CommandDispatcher /memory error paths must not include Exception.message/1 or any raw inspected malformed memory; if rendering fails, output a generic withheld message. Prompt.Assembler memory_summary for canonical malformed memory does not raise and does not expose raw secrets. Regression tests cover malformed whole-field tuple/scalar/map inputs in Memory.render/1, /memory display, and provider-bound Assembler.build/4.
close_reason: Closed

title: Security: close memory validation/rendering gaps for malformed nested terms
description: Review of muse-e49 found confirmed memory secret bypasses for malformed nested terms. Muse.Memory.validate_no_secrets/1 only treats 2-tuples as key/value pairs when the first element is an atom, so {"api_key", "plain-text-value"} currently validates as safe. Rendering/provider-bound paths also inspect tuples after generic redaction, so {:password, "hunter2"} in a canonical memory artifact or arbitrary map can appear raw in Memory.render/1 and Prompt.Assembler memory_summary content. A related render gap exists for malformed maps in artifact list fields other than :open_issues: render/1 applies term-aware redaction only to :open_issues, while other list fields are inspect-string redacted, which can partially or fully leak sensitive-key values such as %{password: "hunter two"} or %{"secret" => "abc def"}. These gaps weaken the muse-e49 guarantee that sensitive atom/string keys block or redact values regardless of value regex matching and that memory rendering is defense-in-depth safe for unsafe stored memory.
acceptance_criteria: Memory.validate_no_secrets/1 flags sensitive atom and string keys in tuple/key-value pair shapes, including nested tuples and list-contained pairs. Memory.render/1 uses recursive key-aware redaction/formatting for all memory artifact fields, not only :open_issues, and never emits raw values associated with sensitive keys in maps, keyword lists, tuples, or malformed nested terms. Prompt.Assembler memory_summary never emits those raw values for canonical memory artifacts or arbitrary map memory. The /memory command remains non-crashing and withholds or safely redacts malformed stored memory. Regression tests cover atom tuple, string tuple, nested tuple, non-open_issues map list fields with atom and string sensitive keys, arbitrary map provider-bound rendering, and /memory display behavior. Avoid dynamic atom creation for generated path/index segments while touching the recursive validator.
close_reason: All five confirmed security bypasses fixed with regression tests. See commit 7f7b931.

title: Security: harden memory artifact secret validation and rendering
description: Security review of closed PR21 (muse-1ki.4.2) found that Muse.Memory.validate_no_secrets/1 only applies regex checks to binary values and recurses maps/lists without treating sensitive keys as unsafe. Nested sensitive-key values such as %{open_issues: [%{api_key: "plain-secret"}]} or non-string/rendered values such as tuples/charlists can pass validation and later be exposed by Muse.Memory.render/1 or prompt memory layers. /memory also renders stored session memory directly via format_memory/1 without re-validating or redacting. Fix by using the shared redactor/sanitizer recursively, checking sensitive keys, normalizing non-string values/rendered output before approval, making compact/persist boundaries fail closed, and adding negative tests for atom/string sensitive keys, deep nesting, lists, tuples/charlists, and /memory display.
close_reason: Closed

title: Security: avoid atom creation from /handoff target input
description: Security review of closed PR21 (muse-1ki.4.2) found that lib/muse/command_dispatcher.ex:1656-1666 parses user-controlled /handoff args with String.to_atom/1. Any unknown target creates a permanent atom before registry validation, allowing atom-table exhaustion via repeated /handoff <unique> inputs. Fix by validating string target IDs through MuseRegistry.fetch/1 or String.to_existing_atom/1 after normalization, returning usage/not-found without creating atoms. Add regression tests for unknown handoff targets and long/unique input.
close_reason: Closed

title: Investigate order-dependent SessionServer remote approval test flake

title: a11y: expose slash-command autocomplete suggestions to screen readers
description: The CommandConsole hook creates a slash-command autocomplete dropdown with role=listbox and role=option, but the textarea (combobox input) lacks required ARIA attributes to expose the relationship to assistive technologies per WAI-ARIA Combobox pattern. Missing on textarea: aria-controls pointing to suggestion box id, aria-expanded true/false, aria-activedescendant pointing to highlighted option, aria-autocomplete=list. Missing on suggestion box: id. Missing on option items: id. WCAG 4.1.2 Name Role Value. P2 - SR users cannot discover or navigate autocomplete suggestions. Parent: muse-1rq
close_reason: Closed

title: a11y: mobile context sidebar leaves background controls focusable
description:
**Found in:** Supplemental browser/keyboard/ARIA QA (not actual OS screen-reader testing)

**Observation:** At 390px viewport width with the mobile context sidebar open, the backdrop and sidebar are visually layered but background chat controls (textarea, Send button) remain in the focusable DOM order behind the overlay.

**Browser evidence (supplemental QA):**
- Focusable order while sidebar open: skip link, mobile toggle button, sidebar buttons, chat textarea, Send button
- Backdrop element visible with `pointer-events: auto` (not limited to sidebar interaction)
- Click on backdrop did NOT close the sidebar
- Mobile toggle pointer action was intercepted by overlay/sidebar

**Expected behavior:**
- When mobile sidebar acts as overlay, focus should be managed: either trap within sidebar/close controls, or mark background content inert/aria-hidden
- Escape key, backdrop click, and close button behavior should be clear and consistent
- Focus should return appropriately when sidebar is dismissed

**Actual behavior:**
- Background controls remain focusable behind overlay
- Backdrop did not dismiss sidebar during QA test
- Tab navigation can reach hidden controls behind the overlay

**WCAG references:** 2.1.1 Keyboard, 2.4.3 Focus Order, 4.1.2 Name/Role/Value

**Note:** Actual screen-reader behavior still needs human verification with VoiceOver/NVDA.
close_reason: Closed

title: Seal remaining invalid ID user-facing formatting leaks
description: Review of completed muse-4iq found remaining user-facing paths that can echo or crash on raw invalid session/profile IDs. Fix dispatcher/CLI/profile-switch formatting so invalid values are not inspected or echoed, and add regression tests.
acceptance_criteria: Invalid session IDs in dispatcher and CLI stream-printer paths render safe generic messages without raw values; invalid workspace profile names in switch do not echo raw values; formatter handles unexpected input robustly; focused tests cover regressions.
close_reason: Fixed during muse-4iq focused review: sealed remaining dispatcher/CLI/profile-switch invalid ID formatting leaks and added regressions.

title: Validate SessionRouter and SessionServer session IDs before starting runtime processes
description: muse-9sr hardened SessionStore operations against invalid/path-traversal session IDs, but SessionRouter.find_or_start_session/1 still stringifies and starts a SessionServer for arbitrary IDs. Most persistence calls then fail non-fatally, and some paths can crash on match (:ok = SessionStore.append_patch(...)) for invalid IDs. Runtime session IDs should be validated at the boundary, not only at disk write helpers.
acceptance_criteria: SessionRouter rejects nil/empty/dot/path-traversal/overlong session IDs before Registry lookup/start; public submit/status/memory/approval APIs return explicit error tuples for invalid IDs; SessionServer child startup cannot register invalid IDs; tests cover invalid IDs for submit, find_or_start_session, memory clear/set, and patch/session persistence paths.
close_reason: Closed

title: Make memory persistence fail closed through Muse.Memory validation
description: Review of muse-9sr found that /memory compact uses Muse.Memory.compact_safe/2, but lower-level SessionServer.set_memory/2 and SessionStore.save_memory/3 persist redacted maps without invoking Muse.Memory.validate_no_secrets/1 and SessionServer persists errors non-fatally while still updating in-memory state. This weakens the intended Memory Muse validation boundary for future callers and imports.
acceptance_criteria: SessionServer.set_memory/2 or the persistence boundary validates memory artifacts with Muse.Memory.validate_no_secrets/1 before durable write; unsafe memory returns/records an error instead of silently persisting or setting divergent state; SessionStore memory import/save behavior is clearly defined for redaction vs validation; tests cover sensitive keys, secret-like nested strings, invalid memory shape, and persistence write failure behavior.
close_reason: Closed

title: Document Phase 4 session persistence, export/import, retention, and workspace profile boundaries
description: Review of muse-9sr found no public docs added for the new SessionStore export/import/retention/memory persistence and workspace profile commands. Existing docs are stale in places: security.md refers to memory files under .muse/sessions/<id>/memory/ while implementation uses memory.json, and command help originally implied /export session writes a file although current behavior copies JSON to clipboard. Public docs should match the actual behavior and limitations.
acceptance_criteria: README or docs describe where sessions/memory/events/messages/patches are stored; export/import UX is documented accurately including clipboard export and file import; retention options and safety boundaries are documented; workspace profile commands document current behavior and any limitations until real active switching lands; stale memory path references are corrected.
close_reason: Closed

title: Stabilize Patch.from_map atom-count regression test
description:
During focused review of muse-3bh, the full `mix test` gate failed in `test/muse/patch_test.exs:647` (`from_map/1 does not create atoms from unknown JSON keys`). The assertion compares global atom counts and failed both in the full suite and when run alone on this machine, with atom deltas such as 24/41.

This appears unrelated to the provider routing changes and likely reflects first-use/runtime atom allocation around `Patch.from_map/1` dependencies rather than unknown-key atom creation. The test should be stabilized without weakening the unknown JSON key safety guarantee.
acceptance_criteria: The Patch.from_map unknown-key safety test is deterministic when run alone and in the full suite; it still proves unknown JSON keys are not converted into newly-created atoms; full mix test no longer fails on this assertion.
close_reason: Closed

title: Accessibility: fix duplicate main landmarks and add skip links

No rendered skip/bypass link was found despite a CSS class existing, violating WCAG 2.4.1 (Bypass Blocks). See docs/audits/liveview-wcag-2.1-aa-2026-05-06.md findings #2 and #3.

- Exactly one <main> landmark per page

title: Accessibility: fix diagnostics drawer focus management
description: The diagnostics drawer overlay opens but focus remains on the trigger/background element, and obscured controls behind the drawer remain in the tab order before drawer controls. This violates WCAG 2.4.3 (Focus Order) and 2.4.7 (Focus Visible). A decision must be made: modal drawer (focus trap, inert background, restore focus on close, Escape to dismiss) or non-modal drawer (correct DOM order, no obscured focus targets). See docs/audits/liveview-wcag-2.1-aa-2026-05-06.md finding #1.
acceptance_criteria:
- Focus is programmatically moved into drawer on open
- Background controls are either inert or focus is trapped within drawer (modal pattern)
- Escape key closes drawer and restores focus to trigger
- No obscured/hidden controls remain in tab order while drawer is open

title: Add real-browser LiveView smoke with console-error detection
description:
## Objective
Complete the browser-QA portion of muse-po4 with a deterministic Playwright/QA Kitten-compatible smoke that drives a real headless browser against the Muse LiveView UI.

## Context
muse-po4 delivered a useful HTTP/HTML smoke harness, but review found the original acceptance around browser console-error detection and keyboard focus cannot be proven without a real browser. The current docs intentionally mark Playwright as optional; this follow-up makes that missing browser layer explicit instead of relying on the HTTP smoke alone.

## Scope
- Reuse script/liveview-browser-smoke or add a companion script/task that starts Muse with MIX_ENV=smoke and MUSE_PROVIDER=fake on a non-default local port.
- Drive a headless browser non-interactively.
- Fail on browser console errors, page errors, unhandled exceptions, LiveView websocket failures, and failed critical network requests.
- Verify basic keyboard focusability/tab order for the composer and visible controls.
- Keep default mix test fake-provider-first and free of provider secrets.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] CI/dev command exists and runs non-interactively with no manual browser interaction
- [ ] The command launches/uses Muse with MIX_ENV=smoke, MUSE_PROVIDER=fake, and a non-default local port, then tears down cleanly
- [ ] The browser opens / and verifies LiveView connects without websocket/origin errors
- [ ] The run fails on console.error, pageerror, unhandledrejection, and critical failed requests
- [ ] The run verifies at least one keyboard path reaches the message composer and send control
- [ ] Documentation covers prerequisites, command, cleanup, and whether Playwright/QA Kitten is required
notes: Created by elixir-code-critic-ae373e during muse-po4 review. HTTP smoke passes, but it cannot prove no browser console errors or keyboard focus behavior.
close_reason: Implemented Playwright LiveView browser smoke. 8 browser checks pass: no console.error/pageerror/unhandledrejection, LiveView WebSocket connected, command/help discoverability in DOM, keyboard Tab reaches composer input, session/context panel ARIA markers, no visible secrets, ARIA landmarks, page loads. Script: ./script/liveview-browser-smoke-playwright. Prerequisites: npm install && npm run browser:install. Default mix test unchanged. All quality gates pass.

title: Phase 2: add executable LiveView browser QA smoke
description:
## Objective
Add a deterministic Playwright/QA Kitten-compatible browser smoke test that verifies the LiveView home page against a running Muse instance with fake provider.

## Context
muse-w23 implemented core UX accessibility and discoverability hardening, but QA Kitten could not execute browser smokes because there is no infrastructure to launch Muse or Phoenix on a non-default port, wait for readiness, and run Playwright checks. This issue tracks that executable browser QA gap.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A script or Mix task exists that: starts Muse/Phoenix on a non-default port (e.g. 4100) with `MUSE_PROVIDER=fake`, waits for HTTP readiness, then runs Playwright/QA Kitten smoke checks, and tears down cleanly
- [ ] Smoke checks verify at minimum: home page loads (HTTP 200), no browser console errors, basic keyboard-focusable input, `/help` discoverability hint visible, session panel renders with status indicators, no visible secrets in rendered HTML
- [ ] The smoke runs non-interactively in CI/dev (no manual browser interaction required)
- [ ] Default `mix test` suite is unchanged and remains fake-provider-first with no provider secrets
- [ ] Running the browser smoke is documented (command, prerequisites, cleanup)
- [ ] If QA Kitten / Playwright is not yet a project dependency, this task includes adding it or documenting the equivalent

## Dependencies
- Depends on: muse-w23 (Phase 2 epic — core accessibility/discoverability must be in place)
- Depends on: muse-8ay (v0.2.0 epic)
notes: Implementation complete. Files: config/smoke.exs, lib/mix/tasks/muse.smoke.ex, script/liveview-browser-smoke. All 6 smoke checks pass: home page loads, accessibility markers, command discoverability, session/context panel, no visible secrets, keyboard focus indicators. IMPORTANT: This is HTTP/HTML smoke — it verifies server-rendered content but cannot detect browser JS runtime errors or prove keyboard focus behavior in a real browser. Real-browser console-error/focus smoke is tracked by muse-3pq (P2). HTTP smoke orchestration hardening is tracked by muse-1fx (P3). Review by elixir-code-critic-ae373e: HTTP/HTML smoke launches and passes on a free non-default port, but original real-browser/console-error acceptance is not fully covered. Created follow-up muse-3pq for real browser console-error/focus smoke and muse-1fx for orchestration hardening.
close_reason: Implemented executable LiveView browser QA smoke. Files: config/smoke.exs, lib/mix/tasks/muse.smoke.ex, script/liveview-browser-smoke. All 6 checks pass: home page loads, accessibility markers, command discoverability, session/context panel markers, no visible secrets, keyboard focus indicators. Default mix test unchanged (3643 tests, 0 failures). Script is non-interactive and cleans up via trap EXIT. Real-browser console-error detection remains optional Playwright (documented in docs/testing.md §11.7).

title: v0.2.0 Phase 3 — Provider/model routing UX and resilience
description:
## Objective
Improve provider and model routing user experience: health/status commands, actionable error messages, safe retry/backoff, and model listing/validation.

## Scope
- Provider health/status CLI commands: 'muse provider status', 'muse provider test' to verify connectivity
- Provider error UX: map provider errors to user-friendly, actionable messages (no stack traces in CLI)
- Safe retry/backoff logic for transient provider failures
- Model listing and config validation: 'muse provider models' to show available models per provider
- External (real provider) tests exist but are opt-in only — never run by default
- Fake provider remains the default for mix test

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 'muse provider status' reports configured providers and their connectivity health
- [ ] Provider connection errors show actionable messages (e.g., 'Check your API key with: muse auth status')
- [ ] Retry/backoff is implemented safely (bounded, no infinite retries, no secrets in logs)
- [ ] 'muse provider models' lists available models per configured provider
- [ ] External provider tests exist but are tagged/excluded from default test run
- [ ] mix test passes with only fake provider (zero real provider calls)
- [ ] Provider config validation catches common misconfigurations at startup

## Dependencies
- Depends on: muse-8ay (Muse Runtime v0.2.0 epic)

title: v0.2.0 Phase 4 — Persistent memory/session and multi-workspace foundations
description:
## Objective
Build durable local session/memory storage that survives restarts, supports export/import, and enables multi-workspace profiles without leaking state between workspaces.

## Scope
- Durable local session store: JSONL continues to work; verify crash recovery across restarts
- Session retention policy: configurable max sessions, TTL, or LRU eviction
- Session export/import: export a session to a portable file (.muse-session?), import and replay
- Trust boundaries: sessions from different workspaces/projects remain isolated
- Secret-safe migration: no secrets leaked during store migration or format changes
- Workspace profiles: , per-workspace config, session isolation per workspace
- Memory Muse persistence: compacted memories survive restart

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Sessions survive Muse restart and process crash recovery
- [ ] Configurable session retention policy works (max count, age-based eviction)
- [ ] Export session to portable file works without secrets exposure
- [ ] Import session from portable file works and replays correctly
- [ ] Different workspaces have isolated sessions and configs
- [ ]  changes active workspace profile
- [ ] No secrets in exported files or during migration
- [ ] Memory Muse persisted data survives restarts

## Dependencies
- Depends on: muse-8ay (Muse Runtime v0.2.0 epic)
close_reason: Closed

description:

title: PR21: /memory clear ignores context session_id
description: Final PR21 audit found that lib/muse/command_dispatcher.ex routes /memory clear differently from the other PR21 session commands. dispatch(:memory_clear, args, _context) discards the caller context and computes session_id with context_session_id(%{}), so it always clears the default session instead of context[:session_id]. /memory, /memory compact, /handoff, /checkpoints, and /restore all use context_session_id(context). In any non-default session or future external/session-scoped command caller, /memory clear reports success while leaving that session's memory intact and may clear the default session unexpectedly. No production/test code was edited during the audit.
acceptance_criteria: /memory clear uses context_session_id(context) consistently with other PR21 commands; clearing a non-default session clears only that session's memory; default-session behavior remains unchanged; regression test covers non-default session routing and prevents accidental clearing of default.
close_reason: Closed

title: Security: make handoff reason redaction robust for non-binary terms
description: PR21 muse-mlp redacts handoff reason before returning the event spec, but Muse.Conductor.request_handoff/4 currently calls Muse.Prompt.Redactor.redact_text/1 directly on opts[:reason]. That function only accepts binaries. A non-binary reason such as {:password, "tuple-sentinel"} raises FunctionClauseError before a safe event spec is returned. Review command used: MIX_ENV=test mix run --no-start -e with Conductor.request_handoff(..., reason: {:password, "tuple-sentinel"}) returned {:raised, FunctionClauseError, "no function clause matching in Muse.Prompt.Redactor.redact_text/1"}. This leaves the returned-spec safety guarantee incomplete for tuple/list/map reason inputs and creates an avoidable caller crash.
acceptance_criteria: Conductor.request_handoff/4 accepts binary and non-binary reason terms without raising. Returned spec data.reason is safe to inspect and contains no raw values under sensitive tuple/map/keyword keys or secret string patterns for tuple/list/map/deep reason inputs. Safe binary reason text remains preserved. Regression tests cover tuple, keyword/list, map, and deep reason inputs and assert inspect(spec) excludes raw sentinel secrets.
close_reason: Closed

title: Security: sanitize handoff reason/context before event specs
description: Security review of closed PR21 (muse-1ki.4.2) found that Muse.Conductor.request_handoff/4 builds a user-visible event spec with reason copied directly from opts and context sanitized only with MetadataSanitizer. MetadataSanitizer is key-based and depth-limited; at depth > max_depth it falls back to inspect/1, which can include raw nested sensitive values before SessionServer's central event redaction runs. Because request_handoff/4 returns the event spec to callers, any caller logging/testing/persisting the spec before SessionServer.emit_session_event/5 can leak raw handoff reason/context. Fix by applying Muse.Prompt.Redactor.redact_term/1 or EventPayloadRedactor at event-spec construction, redacting reason as well as context, and adding regression tests that assert nested secret keys/values are absent from the returned spec itself.
close_reason: Closed

title: PR21: wire memory/handoff/restore commands into session runtime
description: Code review of closed PR21 (muse-1ki.4.2) found acceptance gaps in the command/runtime integration. SessionServer.status/1 currently returns no :memory, :checkpoints, :artifacts, or :workspace keys, so /memory can hit an unmatched case when a session exists, /checkpoints always reports none, and /memory compact rebuilds a minimal session with /tmp. build_session_from_status/1 passes plans/approvals/checkpoints/artifacts into Session.new/1, but Session.new/1 ignores those options, so compaction loses approved plans, artifacts, checkpoints, and validation data. /handoff only prints success and does not emit/request/complete a Conductor handoff or affect Conductor.select_muse/2 for the next turn. /restore is approval-message-only and has no approval object or restore path. Fix by adding explicit SessionRouter/SessionServer APIs for memory, checkpoint listing, handoff request/completion, and approval-gated restore; update select_muse/2 to honor validated active_muse where appropriate; add command_dispatcher integration tests for live sessions.
close_reason: Implemented wire memory/handoff/restore commands into session runtime. Added SessionServer APIs (get_memory, set_memory, clear_memory, set_active_muse) with handle_call handlers. SessionServer.status/1 now includes :memory, :checkpoints, :artifacts, :workspace. Updated build_turn_session to include full session state including memory/checkpoints/artifacts. Conductor.select_muse/2 honors explicit active_muse from session. SessionRouter added get_memory/set_memory/clear_memory/set_active_muse APIs. CommandDispatcher /memory uses get_memory API, /memory compact uses compact_safe with fail-closed and persists via set_memory, /memory clear via clear_memory, /handoff wires through Conductor.request_handoff + complete_handoff + set_active_muse, /restore loads from Checkpoint.Store with approval gating. 3340 tests pass, 0 failures.

title: ToolLoop patch_proposals are not propagated to maybe_capture_patch_proposal in Conductor.run/3
description:
## Summary

`Conductor.maybe_capture_patch_proposal/3` cannot be triggered through normal `Conductor.run/3` because `ToolLoop` accumulates `patch_proposals` in its result, but `finalize_with_specs/7` does not propagate them into `opts` for `maybe_capture_patch_proposal`.

## Details

1. `ToolLoop.build_result/2` returns `%{..., patch_proposals: state.patch_proposals}` — the data IS correctly accumulated.
2. `Conductor.run/3` calls `finalize_with_specs/7` with an `opts` keyword list that includes `provider_module`, `request`, `bundle`, `tool_loop_provider_state` — but **not** `patch_proposals`.
3. Inside `finalize_with_specs/7`, line 367 calls `maybe_capture_patch_proposal(result, muse, opts)`.
4. `maybe_capture_patch_proposal/3` reads `Keyword.get(opts, :patch_proposals, [])` — which always returns `[]` because `:patch_proposals` was never added to opts.
5. Result: the function always falls through to the catch-all clause on line 845 and returns `result` unchanged.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Add `patch_proposals: tool_loop_result.patch_proposals` to the opts keyword list in both call sites of `finalize_with_specs/7` in `Conductor.run/3` (the `:completed` path at ~line 264 and the `:cancelled` path at ~line 305)
- [ ] Write a test that verifies `Conductor.run/3` with a Coding Muse and tool calls that produce patch proposals correctly triggers `maybe_capture_patch_proposal`
- [ ] Verify session transitions to `:awaiting_patch_approval` after a turn with patch_propose tool calls via `Conductor.run/3`

## Files

- `lib/muse/conductor.ex` — the two `finalize_with_specs/7` call sites in `run/3` (around lines 236-265 and 264-305) need `:patch_proposals` added to the opts keyword list
- `lib/muse/conductor/tool_loop.ex` — `build_result/2` already correctly returns `patch_proposals`

## Labels

`bug`, `pr17-follow-up`, `patch-proposal`
close_reason: Fixed: added patch_proposals: tool_loop_result.patch_proposals to both finalize_with_specs/7 call sites in Conductor.run/3 (completed and cancelled paths). Regression test verifies Conductor.run/3 with Coding Muse + patch_propose tool call through ToolLoop correctly captures pending_patch and transitions session to :awaiting_patch_approval.

title: RequestBuilder should strip/validate URL credentials from base_url
description: During PR12 investigation, RequestBuilder.build_url/1 was found to accept and return URLs with embedded credentials (e.g. https://user:pass@example.test/v1) without stripping or rejecting them. title: Read-only Planning Muse — zero writes/shell E2E

## Summary

title: a11y: diagnostics drawer should preserve pre-existing aria-hidden/inert states
description:
## Problem

The DiagnosticsDrawer hook (`assets/js/app.js`) unconditionally removes `aria-hidden` and `inert` from all sibling elements when the drawer closes. If any sibling already had `aria-hidden="true"` before the drawer opened (e.g., decorative `muse-bg` divs, `#clipboard-handler`), that attribute is stripped on close.

## Evidence

- `_applyInert()` records only the DOM element reference, not the pre-existing attribute state.
- `_removeInert()` calls `el.removeAttribute("aria-hidden")` unconditionally for all entries.
- The MobileSidebar hook has the correct pattern: `_inertPush()` records `{el, hadInert, hadAriaHidden, ariaHiddenValue}` before modification, and `_removeInert()` restores precisely.

## Fix

Adopt the MobileSidebar preservation pattern in DiagnosticsDrawer:
1. Change `_inerted` array entries from plain DOM elements to structured records.
2. Add `_inertPush(el)` that snapshots `hadInert`, `hadAriaHidden`, `ariaHiddenValue`.
3. Update `_removeInert()` to restore pre-existing states instead of unconditionally removing.

## Severity

P3 — The drawer is conditionally rendered and decorative/masked siblings already hidden from AT are unlikely to cause user-visible failures, but it is a WCAG 4.1.2 conformance gap.

## Parent

muse-1rq (WCAG 2.1 AA screen-reader audit)
close_reason: Closed

title: a11y: skip link does not move focus to main content target
description:
Found in: Supplemental browser/keyboard/ARIA QA (not actual OS screen-reader testing)

Observation: The skip link exists, is first in focusable DOM order, becomes visually visible on focus, and updates URL hash to #main-content. However, after activation, document.activeElement became BODY, not #main-content.

Browser evidence: Skip link is first focusable element visible on keyboard focus; clicking/activating correctly updates URL hash to #main-content; document.activeElement after activation: BODY element; #main-content element lacks tabindex=-1.

Expected: Activating skip link should move keyboard focus to main content target. Subsequent Tab navigation should begin from main content area.

Actual: URL hash changes but DOM focus did not land on main content target. Focus returned to BODY element.

Suggested: Add tabindex=-1 to main#main-content or add explicit focus-target logic on skip activation.

WCAG refs: 2.4.1 Bypass Blocks, 2.4.3 Focus Order

Note: Actual screen-reader behavior needs human verification with VoiceOver/NVDA.
close_reason: Closed

title: Harden LiveView HTTP smoke orchestration and option validation
description:
## Objective
Reduce false positives and unclear failures in the HTTP/HTML smoke harness delivered by muse-po4.

## Context
Review confirmed script/liveview-browser-smoke works on a free non-default port, but the script and mix task still have edge cases: the script does not fail fast if the launched server process exits during readiness, it can pass against a stale server already listening on the requested port, MUSE_BROWSER_SMOKE_HOST is used for curl/assertions but not passed to mix muse, and mix muse.smoke ignores OptionParser invalid/positional arguments.

## Scope
- Add a port/process preflight or readiness check that verifies the launched Muse process is still alive and fail clearly if bind/startup fails.
- Avoid passing against an unrelated or stale server on the requested port.
- Make MUSE_BROWSER_SMOKE_HOST behavior explicit: pass --host through, validate it, or remove the advertised override.
- Validate mix muse.smoke options, unknown flags, positional args, and port range before running assertions.
- Preserve current fake-provider/no-secrets behavior.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] script/liveview-browser-smoke fails quickly and prints server logs if mix muse exits before readiness
- [ ] The script cannot report success solely because a stale/unrelated server was already listening on the port
- [ ] Host override behavior is implemented and documented consistently
- [ ] mix muse.smoke rejects invalid ports, unknown flags, and positional arguments with clear errors
- [ ] Existing smoke checks still pass on a free local non-default port
notes: Created by elixir-code-critic-ae373e during muse-po4 review. Not a blocker for the happy path, which passed locally, but important for CI trustworthiness.
close_reason: Hardened LiveView smoke option validation and process orchestration; focused tests and HTTP smoke pass.

title: Phase 2: manual WCAG 2.1 AA / screen-reader audit of LiveView
description:
## Objective
Conduct a manual WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility audit of the Muse LiveView interface with screen-reader testing.

## Context
muse-w23 added programmatic ARIA attributes (roles, labels, live regions, sr-only utilities) and basic a11y marker tests in ExUnit. However, full WCAG 2.1 AA compliance requires manual testing with assistive technologies (VoiceOver, NVDA, keyboard-only navigation) that cannot be automated in ExUnit or Playwright alone. This is a genuine gap outside the executable smoke scope.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Manual audit performed with at least one screen reader (VoiceOver on macOS preferred)
- [ ] Keyboard-only navigation verified: all interactive elements focusable, no keyboard traps, tab order logical
- [ ] Color contrast ratios meet AA minimums (4.5:1 for text)
- [ ] ARIA landmarks verified by assistive tech match the roles added in muse-w23
- [ ] Audit findings documented with severity and remediation steps
- [ ] Any critical findings filed as separate bug issues

## Scope
- LiveView home page (chat panel, context sidebar, toast notifications)
- Patch proposal panel (when visible)
- Diagnostics drawer
- Session status card

## Out of Scope
- TUI/CLI accessibility (terminal-based, different accessibility model)
- Automated Playwright/axe checks (tracked in muse-po4)
notes: 2026-05-07: DECISION — Manual OS-level screen-reader audit (VoiceOver/NVDA) intentionally de-scoped by product decision. All structural/browser a11y child issues (muse-1rq.1–7) are completed and closed. However, this issue should NOT be used as evidence of a manual screen-reader pass. No VoiceOver or NVDA certification was performed. The remaining manual assistive-technology audit was skipped to unblock Phase 6; a dedicated SR audit may be filed as a separate Phase 7+ item if needed later.

title: v0.2.0 Phase 5 — Remote execution approval design spike
description:
## Objective
Design-only spike for remote execution approval. No implementation of remote execution capability. Remote execution remains denied by default throughout v0.2.0.

## Scope
- Threat model document for remote execution (what threats exist, what's in/out of scope)
- Approval model: what approvals would be needed before remote execution could run
- Audit events: what events would remote execution emit and how they'd be tracked
- Runner contract: what the Runner behaviour extension for remote targets would look like
- Design document produced as markdown in docs/ for review and future implementation
- Gating: a second approval (separate from patch/plan approval) required for remote execution

## Non-Goals
- No implementation of remote execution
- No SSH/VPS connection code
- No changes to existing Runner, Policy, or ApprovalGate
- No remote execution in CI/CD

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Threat model document exists in docs/ covering remote execution risks
- [ ] Approval model document describes required gates before remote execution
- [ ] Audit event schema defined for remote execution events
- [ ] Runner contract extension described (what a RemoteRunner behaviour would need)
- [ ] Design document is stored in docs/ as a reference for future implementation
- [ ] Remote execution remains denied by default (no code changes to enable it)

## Dependencies
- Depends on: muse-8ay (Muse Runtime v0.2.0 epic)
- Remote execution remains denied by default regardless of this design spike
close_reason: Design spike complete. Artifact at docs/remote-execution-approval-design.md. Follow-up beads created: muse-0uz (Phase B infra), muse-v4c (Phase C foundations), muse-2mm (Phase D SSH implementation). Branch pushed: work/muse-pr5.
